title: Ports, Protocols, Services, and Traffic Types · Incident Case: Net Ipsec Ah Esp Ike
summary: Build and diagnose a service-access matrix from client, firewall, resolver, server, and capture evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net ipsec ah esp ike increment in a verified protocol and service matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain AH, ESP, IKE, tunnel or transport placement, encryption, integrity, peer negotiation, and NAT interaction.
Compare ICMP, TCP, UDP, and GRE purpose, headers, state, errors, and troubleshooting evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
